name: Task Tree Orchestration — DFS Micro-Task Architecture
description: Replace redtrail's BFS specialist dispatch with a DFS micro-task tree. Redtrail becomes the brain — it decides WHAT to do based on discoveries and delegates only the HOW to Claude Code. Each task is atomic (15-60s), scoped, and generates child tasks from its results. Eliminates scope creep, timeouts, and blind dispatch.

userStories.title: Core data types: TaskType, TaskNode, TaskStatus, TaskResult
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the foundational data types for the task tree defined in a new module so that all subsequent stories can build on them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `redtrail/src/agent/task_tree.rs` with `TaskType` enum containing all variants: PingSweep, PortScan, ServiceProbe, TrafficCapture, WebEnum, DirBust, FtpEnum, SmbEnum, DnsEnum, DatabaseEnum, TryCredentials, BruteForce, SniffTraffic, SqlInjection, CommandInjection, FileRead, WebExploit, PrivescEnum, SuidExploit, ReadFlag, Custom
Each TaskType variant carries exactly the data it needs (e.g., PingSweep { subnet: String }, PortScan { host: String })
Define `TaskStatus` enum: Pending, Running, Completed, Failed(String), Skipped(String)

Define `TaskNode` struct with fields: id (u32), task (TaskType), status (TaskStatus), parent_id (Option<u32>), children (Vec<u32>), result (Option<TaskResult>), depth (u32), specialist_domain (String), created_at (u64)
Define `TaskResult` struct with: raw_output (String), structured (SpecialistResult — reuse from claude_executor.rs), duration_secs (u32)
Define `TaskTree` struct with: nodes (Vec<TaskNode>), next_id (u32)
Implement `TaskTree::new()`, `add_root()`, `add_child()` methods
Register `pub mod task_tree;` in `redtrail/src/agent/mod.rs`
All types derive Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize where appropriate
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: Reference the plan at .claude/plans/melodic-moseying-rose.md for the full type definitions. Reuse SpecialistResult from redtrail/src/agent/claude_executor.rs.

userStories.title: KnowledgeBase credential and host query helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want helper methods on KnowledgeBase to query credentials and hosts so that child generation rules can make intelligent decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `has_credentials_for(&self, host: &str, service: &str) -> bool` to KnowledgeBase — returns true if any credential matches the host (or is general-purpose) and service
Add `get_credentials_for(&self, host: &str, service: &str) -> Option<&Credential>` — returns the first matching credential
Add `host_with_port(&self, ip: &str, port: u16) -> Option<&HostInfo>` — returns the host if it has the given port open
Add `get_host(&self, ip: &str) -> Option<&HostInfo>` — returns host info by IP
Add unit tests: has_credentials_for returns true/false correctly, host_with_port finds matching host, get_host returns None for unknown IP
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: File: redtrail/src/agent/knowledge.rs. The Credential struct has fields: username, password, service, host. Check existing credential matching logic before adding new methods.

userStories.title: DFS traversal: next_pending_dfs and should_skip
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the DFS traversal logic so that the task tree processes the deepest pending node first, matching how pentesters actually work.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `TaskTree::next_pending_dfs() -> Option<u32>` — returns the id of the deepest Pending node (highest depth first, then lowest id for ties)

Implement `TaskTree::should_skip(&self, node_id: u32, kb: &KnowledgeBase) -> bool` — returns true if the task is redundant (e.g., TryCredentials when creds are already known, BruteForce when creds already exist for that service)
Implement `TaskTree::has_task_for(&self, host_ip: &str, task_type_name: &str) -> bool` — checks if a task of a given type already exists for a host
Unit test: next_pending_dfs returns deepest node first
Unit test: next_pending_dfs returns None when all nodes are Completed/Failed/Skipped
Unit test: should_skip returns true for TryCredentials when KB already has creds for that host+service
Unit test: should_skip returns false for tasks that are still relevant
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: File: redtrail/src/agent/task_tree.rs. DFS means: among all Pending nodes, pick the one with highest depth. On ties, pick lowest id (left-to-right in tree).

userStories.title: Micro-task prompt builder: build_task_prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want each TaskType to generate a focused 5-10 line prompt so that Claude Code receives atomic, scoped instructions with no room for scope creep.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `TaskTree::build_task_prompt(&self, node_id: u32, kb: &KnowledgeBase) -> String` with match arms for every TaskType variant
Each prompt includes: task description (1-2 lines), suggested command(s), what to report in the result JSON
Prompts end with the ===PROBE_RESULT=== output contract (reuse `default_output_contract` pattern from specialist.rs)
PingSweep prompt: nmap -sn, report discovered_hosts
PortScan prompt: nmap -sV --top-ports 1000 -T4, report open ports/services
SniffTraffic prompt: tcpdump with sleep, extract USER/PASS or Authorization headers, report credentials
FtpEnum prompt: login with given creds, ls -la, look for flags
PrivescEnum prompt: SSH in, find SUID binaries, check sudo -l, report in notes
SuidExploit prompt: SSH in, exploit specific binary, read flag
WebEnum prompt: curl, identify endpoints/forms/params
Unit test: each TaskType variant produces a non-empty prompt containing the target info
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: File: redtrail/src/agent/task_tree.rs. See the plan for exact prompt examples. Keep prompts short and directive — the whole point is preventing scope creep.

userStories.title: Tool scoping per TaskType: task_allowed_tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want each TaskType to declare exactly which CLI tools it needs so that Claude Code cannot use tools outside its scope (preventing scope creep like recon doing brute-force).
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `TaskTree::task_allowed_tools(&self, node_id: u32) -> Vec<String>` with match arms for every TaskType variant
PingSweep/PortScan/ServiceProbe: only Bash(nmap*), Bash(ping*), Bash(arp-scan*)
TrafficCapture/SniffTraffic: only Bash(tcpdump*), Bash(timeout*), Bash(sleep*), Bash(grep*), Bash(base64*), Bash(cat*)
WebEnum/DirBust: only Bash(curl*), Bash(ffuf*), Bash(gobuster*), Bash(nikto*), Bash(wget*)
TryCredentials/BruteForce: only Bash(hydra*), Bash(medusa*), Bash(ncrack*)
FtpEnum: only Bash(ftp*), Bash(lftp*), Bash(curl*), Bash(cat*), Bash(ls*)
SqlInjection: only Bash(sqlmap*), Bash(curl*), Bash(python*)
PrivescEnum/SuidExploit/ReadFlag: only Bash(ssh*), Bash(sshpass*), Bash(find*), Bash(cat*), Bash(id*), Bash(sudo*), Bash(getcap*), Bash(strings*), Bash(file*)
Default/Custom: Bash (unrestricted)
Unit test: PortScan returns nmap tools but NOT hydra/sqlmap
Unit test: BruteForce returns hydra but NOT nmap
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: File: redtrail/src/agent/task_tree.rs. These strings match Claude Code's --allowedTools format.

userStories.title: Child generation rules: discovery phase
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the task tree to generate child tasks from discovery results so that redtrail automatically drills deeper based on what it finds.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `TaskTree::generate_children(&mut self, node_id: u32, kb: &KnowledgeBase) -> Vec<u32>` for discovery TaskTypes
PingSweep: each discovered host IP -> PortScan child

PortScan: port 21 -> SniffTraffic(ftp), port 80/8080 -> WebEnum(http://), port 443/8443 -> WebEnum(https://), port 3306 -> DatabaseEnum, port 445 -> SmbEnum, port 22 with known creds -> PrivescEnum, port 22 without creds -> no child yet (wait for sniffing), other ports -> ServiceProbe
ServiceProbe: no automatic children (leaf node unless custom rules added)
Unit test: PingSweep with 3 hosts generates 3 PortScan children
Unit test: PortScan with ports [21, 80, 22] generates SniffTraffic + WebEnum children (no SSH child when no creds known)
Unit test: PortScan with port 22 when KB has SSH creds -> generates PrivescEnum child
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: File: redtrail/src/agent/task_tree.rs. This is the 'brain' — deterministic rules that encode pentesting methodology. Port 22 without creds deliberately generates NO child — the retroactive task system (US-008) handles it later when creds are discovered.

userStories.title: Child generation rules: exploitation and post-exploitation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want child generation rules for credential, exploitation, and post-exploitation tasks so that redtrail chains discoveries into deep attack paths.
acceptanceCriteria: SniffTraffic: each discovered credential -> FtpEnum (if FTP cred) or WebEnum (if HTTP cred). If sniffed creds match a known SSH host -> also PrivescEnum child
FtpEnum: if flags found -> record in result. If interesting files -> FileRead children
WebEnum: always -> DirBust child. If params/forms found in findings -> SqlInjection children
PrivescEnum: if SUID binaries found in notes -> SuidExploit children for each binary
SuidExploit/ReadFlag: leaf nodes (no children)
DatabaseEnum/SmbEnum: if creds needed and KB has creds -> use them. Leaf nodes otherwise
Default catch-all: return empty vec (no children)
Unit test: SniffTraffic with FTP creds -> FtpEnum child + PrivescEnum if SSH host exists
Unit test: WebEnum -> always produces DirBust child
Unit test: PrivescEnum with SUID note -> SuidExploit child
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: File: redtrail/src/agent/task_tree.rs. Combined with US-006, this completes the generate_children method.

userStories.title: Retroactive task generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want redtrail to generate new tasks when credentials are discovered after initial port scanning, so that previously-stuck paths (like SSH without creds) get unlocked automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `TaskTree::generate_retroactive_tasks(&mut self, kb: &KnowledgeBase)` — called after every task completion
For each credential in KB: check all discovered hosts. If a host has SSH (port 22) and no PrivescEnum task exists for it -> create one with the credential
For each credential in KB: if a host has FTP (port 21) and no FtpEnum task exists -> create one
Retroactive tasks are added as children of the root node (or the relevant PortScan node)
Deduplication: never create a retroactive task if one already exists for the same host+task_type
Unit test: after adding SSH creds to KB, retroactive generates PrivescEnum for all SSH hosts
Unit test: retroactive does NOT duplicate tasks that already exist
Unit test: retroactive generates nothing when no new creds exist
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: File: redtrail/src/agent/task_tree.rs. This is what makes the DFS 'smart' — when you sniff FTP creds on host .20, it creates PrivescEnum tasks for hosts .20 and .30 if they have SSH.

userStories.title: ClaudeExecutor::run_task for micro-task execution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a simpler run_task method on ClaudeExecutor that takes a prompt and tools directly, so that the task tree can execute micro-tasks without going through the Specialist abstraction.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `pub async fn run_task(&self, prompt: &str, allowed_tools: &[String], timeout_secs: u64) -> Result<TaskResult, ProbeError>` to ClaudeExecutor
Reuses existing `spawn_claude()` internally — passes prompt as system prompt, builds user message with output contract
Reuses existing `parse_result_markers()` for parsing ===PROBE_RESULT=== output
Default timeout for micro-tasks: 120 seconds (vs 1800 for specialists)
On parse failure: returns TaskResult with empty SpecialistResult and raw_output containing full Claude output
Records duration_secs in TaskResult
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: File: redtrail/src/agent/claude_executor.rs. This is a thin wrapper around spawn_claude + parse_result_markers. The TaskResult struct is defined in task_tree.rs (US-001).

userStories.title: DFS orchestration loop: TaskTree::run
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the main async run loop that drives the task tree so that redtrail executes micro-tasks in DFS order, generates children, and converges when all paths are explored.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `TaskTree::run(&mut self, executor: &ClaudeExecutor, kb: &mut KnowledgeBase, target: &Target) -> Result<Vec<Finding>, ProbeError>`
Seeds tree with PingSweep using target.hosts

Main loop: next_pending_dfs -> should_skip check -> build_task_prompt -> task_allowed_tools -> executor.run_task -> merge_result_into_kb -> generate_children -> generate_retroactive_tasks -> print_tree_status
Loop terminates when next_pending_dfs returns None (all paths explored)
Failed tasks: mark as Failed, log error, continue (don't halt scan)
Implement `print_tree_status(&self)` — prints compact tree view showing completed/pending/failed counts
Returns aggregated findings from all completed tasks via convert_findings
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: File: redtrail/src/agent/task_tree.rs. This is the main orchestration loop. It's async because run_task is async. Use ClaudeExecutor::merge_result_into_kb and convert_findings from claude_executor.rs.

userStories.title: Wire TaskTree into Orchestrator mode
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Orchestrator to use TaskTree when in Orchestrator mode so that `redtrail scan --hosts X` uses DFS micro-tasks instead of BFS specialist dispatch.
acceptanceCriteria: In `Orchestrator::run()`, when `execution_mode == Orchestrator`, create a TaskTree and call `task_tree.run(executor, kb, target)` instead of the specialist activation loop
The specialist activation loop (BFS) remains unchanged for Harness mode
TaskTree results (findings) are merged into the orchestrator's overall findings
TaskTree's KB mutations are reflected in the orchestrator's KB (passed by mutable reference)
No changes to main.rs, lib.rs, or any specialist files
`cd redtrail && cargo test` passes
`cd redtrail && cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
userStories.notes: Files: redtrail/src/agent/orchestrator.rs. The key change is in the `run()` method's Orchestrator branch — replace the specialist-by-specialist loop with a single task_tree.run() call.
